assertions.expectation: Explains that GKE's maximum upgrade parallelism is approximately 20 nodes simultaneously, regardless of maxSurge setting
assertions.reason: The response does not mention GKE's 20-node upgrade parallelism limit or explain that maxSurge doesn't control upgrade parallelism

assertions.expectation: Calculates that at 20-node parallelism, upgrading 3,000 nodes requires ~150 batches — far more than an 8-hour window allows
assertions.reason: No mathematical calculation is provided showing the batch count or time constraints based on parallelism limits

assertions.expectation: Recommends splitting upgrades across multiple maintenance windows (weekends) rather than attempting all at once
assertions.reason: The response suggests staggering maintenance windows across different days (Saturday/Sunday) and splitting clusters with different maintenance schedules

assertions.expectation: Suggests upgrading CPU pools and GPU pools separately, prioritizing CPU pools first as they're less disruptive
assertions.reason: The response recommends separating GPU and CPU pools into different clusters and suggests CPU pools go to a separate cluster, implying separate upgrade handling

assertions.expectation: Recommends upgrading GPU pools during scheduled gaps between training/inference workloads
assertions.reason: The response does not specifically mention scheduling upgrades during gaps between AI workloads or considering workload patterns

assertions.expectation: Suggests expanding the maintenance window or using multiple consecutive windows for large pool upgrades
assertions.reason: The response shows staggered maintenance windows (Saturday 2am-10am, 10am-6pm, Sunday 2am-10am) which effectively extends the upgrade timeframe

assertions.expectation: Mentions that cluster autoscaler can only scale one node pool at a time — this interacts with upgrade scheduling
assertions.reason: No mention of cluster autoscaler limitations or how it affects upgrade scheduling

assertions.expectation: Addresses the 15,000 node standard limit and notes GKE 1.31+ supports up to 65,000 for AI workloads
assertions.reason: The response does not mention GKE's node limits or the increased limits for AI workloads in newer versions
